transcript: Electronics Boutique, where you can trade in your old games, this is Jeff.
Umm... I need to make a... [delayed] couple o' upgrades. It's, it's a... digital repressor.
Hmm. Oh... oh, oh, is your-- you--
Yeah. [echoes, increases in pitch] Pretty much from... from, uh, ground up. Ya know?
M'kay. Ummm, we actually mainly deal with--
Reason I ask... no, reason I ask is 'cause the guy at the Sunglass Hut... was talkin' about you...
...so I'd probably... I'd probably check it out.
Reason being, ya know, is just 'cause of that. [echoes and increases in pitch]
Um, we mostly deal with software. Um, if you want to upgrade the entire system--
It was during a chess... session. [echoes, decreases in pitch] He was... it was digital chess.
Chess! [echoes, increases then slowly decreases in pitch]
Let's see here... yeah, uh... we... we have, uh... mainly software. If you're looking for the...
...hardware stuff, uh.. I'd say probably, uh...
...CompUSA would probably be the best place to look for it.
'Kay. 'Cause I thought so. [echoes, increases in pitch] But that repressor... how much repression is there, in something like that?
[deep breath] To tell ya the truth, I do not know, I'm... I'm not exactly familiar with... uh, uh... the newer, uh, pretty much any of the word processors on the market.. umm...
[sigh] Right. That's true. [echoes loudly] Because it's just one of those things...
["one of those things" repeats in a low pitch]
CompUSA would probably be the best place to look.
["Once something's nebulized" repeats in a high pitch]
["Once something's rendered" repeats in a high pitch]
["Once something's rendered" repeats in a high pitch]
Once I've rendered something nebulized... I've realized... [echoes in a lower pitch]
["I've real--" repeats in a low pitch]
That... the repression of the simulated nebulizer... [echoes, increasing in pitch] ...is a repression... [echoes, decreasing in pitch] ...of the digital chess session. [echoes in a lowered pitch]
Where you guys stand on somethin' like that?
C'mon, talk to me a little bit. Appreciate your time.
Now, I certainly do appreciate it. Certainly do.
["Now, I certainly do" repeats in extremely low pitch]
Now, let me have it now as a representation... of that nebulized simulation. [echoes, decreasing in pitch]
Uh, so you're... I mean, we have some chess software, but not a who... whole lot of it.
We have, like, the chetmat... Chessmaster stuff, but we don't carry any of the... uh... the physical chess boards.
["Are they sim--" repeats in a low pitch]
Well, you... you can do the--
